predictions.text: By 2026-04-15, the White House will issue a formal executive action to ensure TSA agents are paid during the DHS funding lapse (e.g., Executive Order, Presidential Memorandum, or DHS/OMB directive publicly posted) explicitly referencing TSA pay.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verification if an Executive Order, Presidential Memorandum, or an official DHS/OMB public directive is issued by the White House/DHS/OMB stating TSA agents will be paid (or receive targeted pay/advance/exception) during the DHS funding lapse, with TSA explicitly named.
evidence: DHS funding lapse becomes longest partial shutdown; Trump vows executive action to pay TSA agents. https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/26/trump-tsa-shutdown-dhs.html
monitoring_signals: WhiteHouse.gov Presidential Actions page
DHS press releases and TSA newsroom updates
OMB memoranda or guidance posted on whitehouse.gov/omb
Major-wire reporting (Reuters/AP) confirming the mechanism
predictions.rationale: Public commitment plus operational risk at airports creates pressure for a narrowly tailored executive workaround even during a funding lapse.

predictions.text: By 2026-04-30, Congress will enact (passed by both chambers and signed) a DHS appropriations measure or continuing resolution that restores DHS funding.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verification if a bill funding DHS (full-year appropriation or CR) becomes Public Law by 2026-04-30, evidenced by Congress.gov status showing 'Became Public Law' and the statute provides DHS budget authority.
evidence: DHS funding lapse becomes longest partial shutdown, highlighting fiscal brinkmanship. https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/26/trump-tsa-shutdown-dhs.html
monitoring_signals: Congress.gov bill status for DHS funding vehicles
House/Senate floor schedules and leadership statements
OMB apportionment/resumption notices
News alerts from Reuters/AP/CQ Roll Call
predictions.rationale: Extended shutdowns typically trigger mounting political and operational costs, increasing odds of a CR or compromise within weeks.

predictions.text: On or before 2026-04-06, the U.S. President will publicly extend the pause on strikes against Iranian energy facilities beyond 2026-04-06 (to a stated date at least 7 days later).
predictions.verification_criteria: Verification if a White House/DoD statement, presidential remarks transcript, or official briefing explicitly extends the strike pause on Iranian energy facilities beyond April 6 and specifies a new end date at least 7 days after April 6.
evidence: Trump extends pause on strikes on Iranian energy facilities to April 6 amid market stress. https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/26/trump-iran-war-oil-energy-pause.html
monitoring_signals: White House press briefings and readouts
DoD press briefings and CENTCOM updates
Reuters/CNBC/NYT follow-up reporting on strike rules of engagement
Publicly stated timelines in presidential remarks
predictions.rationale: If markets are already discounting de-escalation messaging, an explicit extension offers a low-cost signal to limit oil shock risk.

predictions.text: By 2026-04-25, the U.S. Department of Defense will file a notice of appeal (or seek an emergency stay) of the Anthropic-related preliminary injunction referenced in recent reporting.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verification if a notice of appeal is filed on the court docket (PACER/RECAP) by DoD (or DOJ on DoD’s behalf), or if a motion for stay pending appeal/emergency stay is filed, explicitly tied to the injunction described in reporting.
evidence: Court grants Anthropic a preliminary injunction blocking a Pentagon 'supply chain risk' label. https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/26/anthropic-pentagon-dod-claude-court-ruling.html
monitoring_signals: PACER/RECAP docket entries for the case
DoJ Civil Division / DoD statements
Appellate docket in the relevant Circuit
Reporting citing government litigation strategy
predictions.rationale: Preliminary injunctions affecting procurement and labeling authority are commonly appealed quickly to preserve discretion and avoid precedent.

predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, the FHFA will publish formal written guidance (advisory, directive, bulletin, or public letter) addressing underwriting/eligibility treatment of crypto-related collateral or crypto-backed mortgage structures for at least one GSE (Fannie Mae or Freddie Mac).
predictions.verification_criteria: Verification if FHFA posts a dated guidance document on FHFA.gov (or a public letter to the Enterprises) that explicitly discusses crypto-backed mortgage products, crypto as collateral, or crypto custody/valuation requirements for GSE underwriting/eligibility.
evidence: Fannie Mae accepts its first crypto-backed mortgage product, signaling mainstreaming of digital assets in housing finance. https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/26/fannie-mae-accepts-first-crypto-backed-mortgage-product.html
monitoring_signals: FHFA directives/advisories page and press releases
Fannie/Freddie selling guide update logs
Congressional inquiries/hearings mentioning GSE crypto exposure
Industry commentary from MBA/NAHB referencing FHFA rules
predictions.rationale: A first-of-kind GSE product tends to trigger supervisory clarification on valuation, custody, and liquidity assumptions.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, Freddie Mac will publicly confirm acceptance of at least one crypto-backed mortgage structure (or an approved pilot) via a Selling Guide bulletin, official press release, or FHFA-confirmed program update.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verification if Freddie Mac posts a Selling Guide Bulletin or official announcement explicitly stating that a crypto-backed mortgage product/structure is eligible/accepted (pilot or general), or FHFA publicly confirms such acceptance for Freddie Mac.
evidence: Fannie Mae accepts its first crypto-backed mortgage product, raising the chance of competitive/parallel GSE adoption. https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/26/fannie-mae-accepts-first-crypto-backed-mortgage-product.html
monitoring_signals: Freddie Mac Selling Guide Bulletins and updates
FHFA statements on GSE innovation and risk management
Mortgage fintech announcements naming Freddie participation
Trade press (Inside Mortgage Finance, HousingWire) confirmations
predictions.rationale: If FHFA tolerates the risk controls for one Enterprise, pressure increases for harmonized policy or a controlled Freddie pilot.

predictions.text: By 2026-09-30, at least one U.S. city/county will enact a moratorium (temporary prohibition) on new data center construction or permits specifically citing grid/power, water, or environmental strain, and the moratorium will apply to facilities above 50 MW (or described as 'hyperscale' or 'large-load').
predictions.verification_criteria: Verification if an official local-government order/ordinance/resolution is posted (or covered by major local press) establishing a moratorium on new data center approvals, with the text or coverage explicitly citing infrastructure strain (power/water) and defining scope as >50 MW or hyperscale/large-load.
evidence: AI data-center buildout faces local permitting backlash and looming power scrutiny; Meta’s large Texas project highlights constraints. https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/26/meta-to-spend-10-billion-on-ai-data-center-in-el-paso-1gw-by-2028.html
monitoring_signals: City/county council agendas in major data-center corridors (TX, VA, AZ, GA, OH)
Local utility interconnection queues and public hearings
State PUC filings referencing large-load impacts
Trade press tracking moratoria (Data Center Dynamics, WSJ/Reuters local angles)
predictions.rationale: Rapid hyperscale load growth commonly triggers short-term permitting pauses while jurisdictions update zoning, utility, and water requirements.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, the U.S. Department of Energy will publish an RFI (Request for Information), formal study, or strategy document explicitly focused on data center electricity demand growth and grid impacts, with AI/data centers named prominently in the title or executive summary.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verification if DOE releases on energy.gov or Federal Register an RFI, report, or strategy document where 'data centers' and 'AI' (or 'artificial intelligence') are explicitly named as a major driver of electricity demand/grid planning challenges in the title, executive summary, or purpose section.
evidence: AI data-center buildout faces looming power scrutiny; large projects highlight grid constraints. https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/26/meta-to-spend-10-billion-on-ai-data-center-in-el-paso-1gw-by-2028.html
monitoring_signals: Federal Register notices from DOE
DOE Office of Electricity and EIA releases mentioning data centers
Congressional letters directing DOE study work
Interagency grid reliability statements referencing large loads
predictions.rationale: Sustained public attention to large-load interconnections makes a DOE fact-finding process and guidance document politically and operationally likely.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, the U.S. FDA will issue a public communication (Safety Communication, guidance, or workshop summary) explicitly warning about or addressing deepfake/manipulated medical images (e.g., X-rays/CT/MRI) in clinical workflows or fraud, naming 'deepfake' or 'synthetic' images as a risk.
predictions.verification_criteria: Verification if FDA posts (FDA.gov) a safety communication, guidance, or official workshop/meeting output that explicitly mentions 'deepfake' or 'synthetic/manipulated medical images' as a clinical or fraud risk and discusses mitigation (provenance, detection, standards).
evidence: Deepfake X-rays now look real even to doctors, raising fraud/misdiagnosis risks and need for provenance/detection standards. https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/03/260326011452.htm
monitoring_signals: FDA CDRH safety communications and AI/ML guidance updates
FDA public workshops on digital health or imaging devices
Major fraud case reporting involving synthetic radiology images
Standards activity (NIST, DICOM committees) referenced by FDA
predictions.rationale: As synthetic medical-image capability becomes widely documented, regulator attention to provenance and device-workflow safeguards tends to follow.

predictions.text: By 2026-07-31, New York State will report year-to-date Personal Income Tax (PIT) collections at least $2.0B above the state budget forecast for the same period, attributed in part to Wall Street/finance income (bonuses, capital gains, or withholding).
predictions.verification_criteria: Verification if an official NYS DOB monthly cash report, quarterly update, or Comptroller release shows PIT receipts >= $2.0B above forecast for the comparable period, and the narrative notes finance-sector/Wall Street-related drivers (bonuses/capital gains/withholding).
evidence: Wall Street bonuses total $49.2B, implying near-term boost to New York tax receipts. https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/26/nyregion/wall-street-bonus.html
monitoring_signals: NYS Division of the Budget monthly cash reports
NY State Comptroller revenue updates
NYC OMB revenue reports (for corroboration)
Bank earnings compensation disclosures and bonus pool estimates
predictions.rationale: Large bonus pools typically lift PIT withholding and estimated payments, though market volatility can offset via lower capital gains.
